I read the prompt‚Äôs submission constraints and the prior-turn log as external work, not as proof. The prompt requires the first two generators to be the tame generators sigma,tau, with the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 implicit, later generators in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs surviving proposal is still the dynamic tame-inertia candidate; earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The still-live candidate is:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ, ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,with ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant in this word, because that exponent is only applied to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

What I regard as genuinely established The following prior results look solid as candidate-side computations, provided one accepts the finite linear algebra in the log.First, the two recurring quotient conventions should be frozen as equations, not informal fractions.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, with t the deck involution of V=kerőļ and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character, the Fox coefficient forNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄisDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j,and it satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít.ThusDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument work: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, so the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Therefore nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0 up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1 convention. The legitimate elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.

Do not invert this relation inside F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one gets (1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1)for any nontrivial f-th root ő∂. Since f=2a m, s‚Č°1(mod2a), s‚Č°0(modm), the integer 2s+1 is coprime to f. So h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero.With these conventions, the candidate-side abelianization statements remain coherent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.The finite-tame cup-product calculations and the sign-kernel computations are meaningful evidence. They are not, by themselves, a proof of the full absolute Galois presentation.New Turn 57 contribution: the remaining obstruction is a slice torsor, not a raw coordinate

The latest prior turns correctly observed that H1(P,L2H) classifies differences of marked Frobenius lifts, not the absolute first IA term of one lift. I want to sharpen that into a precise ‚Äúslice torsor‚ÄĚ formulation.

Fix a finite tame quotientőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©.

Write f=2a m with m odd, let

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©,P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a,and let S=ŌÉf be the marked pro-2 unramified element. In the reduced degree-one model,L1‚ČÉF2 [őĒ]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S.

A marked lift őĪ of the P-generator satisfiesőĪ2a=Inn(S).Ifmb=őĪ(b)b‚ąí1‚ąąL2H,then expanding őĪ2a (b)=SbS‚ąí1 in L2 gives the affine norm equationNP(mb)=[S,b].

Thus mb is not itself a cohomology class. The homogeneous cohomology groupH1(P,L2H)=kerNP/im(őĪ‚ąí1)only classifies differences mb‚Ä≤‚ąímb between two marked lifts with the same S.Now let the actual candidate Schreier relations around the P-cycle be written to degree two asőĪ(bp)=bp+1 exp(őėp)+O(L3),p‚ąąP,where őėp‚ąąL2H is the p-slice of the X-only quadratic part of the candidate‚Äôs actual relation. Multiplying around the P-cycle gives p‚ąąP‚ąĎőėp=[S,b].Equivalently, the candidate‚Äôs actual quadratic relation has the formqact=Qact+[b,S],Qact=p‚ąąP‚ąĎőėp.

So the candidate itself hasmbcand=őė0 after the same H-equivariant lift of b. Consequentlymbcand‚ąíőė0=0 is tautological once őė0 is chosen as the candidate‚Äôs own affine origin.The key point is that knowing only the total quadratic relation qact, or equivalently the cup-product form, does not determine the slice őė0. Different slice choices with the same total sum differ by elements of kerNP, hence by classes in H1(P,L2H) after quotienting by changes of b-lift. This explains why the prior rank, cup, abelianization, sign-kernel, and cyclotomic tests can all pass while the filtered operator action remains undecided.So the decisive comparison is not ‚Äúwhat are the candidate‚Äôs raw seven coordinates in (e,f)=(5,4)?‚ÄĚ The candidate‚Äôs normalized residual is zero by construction if one uses its own qact -slice as origin. The decisive comparison is:[mbarith‚ąíőė0cand]‚ąąH1(P,L2H).That is the first meaningful arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction.For (e,f)=(5,4), this class lives in the seven-dimensional space previously identified: two square directions and five involution-indexed commutator directions. But after the above normalization, the candidate contributes zero; the entire problem is to compute whether arithmetic also lands at that same affine origin.

Practical next arithmetic target

For the degree-20 tame field one can take the standard modelL=Q2(ő∂5,ŌĖ),ŌĖ5=2,with ŌĄ(ŌĖ)=ő∂5 ŌĖ,ŌÉ(ő∂5)=ő∂52,ŌÉ(ŌĖ)=ŌĖ.This fixes the Frobenius-lift gauge. The first nonabelian obstruction should be computed as follows.Choose the same H-equivariant lift of the invariant Kummer class b on the arithmetic side. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5. Subtract the candidate slice őė0cand coming from the actual candidate quadratic relation, not from a normalized square-only DemuŇ°kin form. The target is vanishing inH1(P,L2H).This is a sharper task than another candidate-side computation, because candidate residuals vanish after candidate-defined normalization. Only arithmetic-minus-candidate residuals can still refute or confirm the word.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. The stable algebraic conventions areDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.New this turn: the first IA obstruction is best formulated as a slice torsor. H1(P,L2H) classifies arithmetic-minus-candidate differences of marked Frobenius lifts, not raw coordinates of a single lift. Candidate residuals vanish after using the candidate‚Äôs own qact -slice as affine origin.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing step is an arithmetic comparison or dyadic formation theorem proving that the actual local Galois action has the same filtered slice origins as the candidate over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), use L=Q2(ő∂5,ŌĖ), ŌĖ5=2, with ŌÉ(ŌĖ)=ŌĖ, to compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate class in H1(P,L2H) using the same H-equivariant b-lift and the candidate‚Äôs actual qact -slice. The target is zero.[BREAKTHROUGH]
